title: The Chanukiah vs. the Menorah: Understanding the Difference
description: Clarifying the difference between the seven-branched Temple menorah and the nine-branched Chanukah chanukiah — their origins, meanings, and why the distinction matters.

en.title: The Chanukiah vs. the Menorah: Understanding the Difference

zh.title: 光明节烛台（Chanukiah）与圣殿烛台（Menorah）：理解其区别

cultural_parallels: Chinese Lantern Festival (元宵节) and light celebrations
Yin and yang (阴阳) complementarity - memory and hope
Public light displays and community celebrations

historical_context.chinese_equivalent_temple: Western Zhou Dynasty (西周, 1046-771 BCE) to early Han Dynasty (汉朝, 206 BCE - 220 CE)
historical_context.significance: Distinction between Temple-era worship and post-Temple Jewish resilience

key_topics: Seven-branched Temple menorah specifications and symbolism
Nine-branched Chanukah chanukiah structure and purpose
Maccabean revolt and miracle of the oil
Why exact menorah replicas are prohibited
Lighting customs and laws for each
Cultural parallels with Chinese light festivals
Evolution from centralized Temple worship to home-based practice

temple_menorah.material: Single piece of hammered gold (34 kg / 75 lbs)
temple_menorah.replication: Forbidden by Jewish law out of respect

chanukiah.materials: Any material (silver, brass, ceramic, ice, etc.)
chanukiah.placement: Window or doorway to publicize the miracle
chanukiah.requirement: Eight lights at equal height plus elevated shamash

author: Torah Light Project
target_audience: Chinese native Mandarin speakers with no prior Jewish knowledge
